text:
Once there was a quiet town named Slightburg. The locals loved its serene landscapes, mild temperatures year-round, and the gentle hoot of the owl that dwelled in the highest tower of Slightburg’s old, oak-covered church. The town’s only business was a general store run by Mr. Jasper Blencowe, who had weathered more winters than any of his customers could count. On summer evenings, when the sun dipped behind the whispering pines, everyone gathered in the cozy main square, where everyone knew your name and your favorite biscuit recipe.

One seemingly ordinary day, a girl named Patricia stumbled into Slightburg. She was adventurous, having come from a city where skyscrapers were considered quaint. Patricia’s eyes swept across the quaint shops and farms that lined the narrow streets, wondering why this peaceful place was so special. Her curiosity, however, led her to a bookshop tucked behind the general store.
